summary: We are in full execution mode for the China Going-Global report, Intelligent China launch, and Asia Start campaign, with key deadlines this week and next.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint report with IE Business School on Chinese companies going global, targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore. This week, Felix must meet with both partners to confirm event themes and launch feasibility, while the team begins translation of the Chinese report and creates a one-pager proposal. Concurrently, the Intelligent China program website must launch this week, and the SCM campaign should start immediately, as this is a new flagship program targeting foreigners. The Asia Start campaign is running two routes (AI vs traditional) for the first month, with a decision point approaching. Urgent this week: Joseph needs to fix Asia Start ad targeting, launch the Intelligent China website, and provide conversion tracking setup. Julie must adjust the Shun Tian Shu press release and send the Asia Start spotlight article. Xuan needs to follow up with agencies (Carnegie, Impactable) and join the Google program brochure meeting. Next week, key reviews include the knowledge page revamp, RFP for social media agency, and translated articles for media pitching. The US partnerships are on hold as we focus on Asia Star and Intelligent China.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to improve search rankings for China programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and agency prioritization.

meetings.title: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: The team discusses a joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and a launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Campaign Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' for December. Need to launch website and plan SCM campaign.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping cooperation.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; content optimization can begin.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to budget constraints and time.
The report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School, with a main research piece from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools' leadership and industry practitioners.

decisions.text: Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.

decisions.text: Target launch in mid-September at either Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore, with digital version ready by early September.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Asia Start campaign will run two routes for first month: Joseph using AI, Daisy using traditional strategy, then decide on one.
